title: Market Brief — Sunday, June 28, 2026
summary: GPU compute is loose across every class: util-avail stands at 36% for A100, 35% for H100, 37% for H200, and 37% for B200, all signaling ample spare capacity well above scarcity thresholds. The biggest overnight move is H200 spot jumping 10.3% to $3.45/hr, a demand spike contradicting the broader utilization glut where H200 usage cratered 31.3pp over 30 days.
paragraphs: GPU compute is loose across every class: util-avail stands at 36% for A100, 35% for H100, 37% for H200, and 37% for B200, all signaling ample spare capacity well above scarcity thresholds. The biggest overnight move is H200 spot jumping 10.3% to $3.45/hr, a demand spike contradicting the broader utilization glut where H200 usage cratered 31.3pp over 30 days.

DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is selling below its TCO-adjusted B200 production cost of $4.00/M, burning roughly $3.66 per million tokens to hold market share. OpenAI grades Neutral, monetizing o3 Pro at $80/M output while subsidizing gpt-oss-120b at $0.15/M. Anthropic grades Strong on Opus 4.6 premium pricing. Google grades Neutral. DeepSeek grades Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $4.38/hr looks overvalued given 65% of NeoCloud offerings sitting idle and utilization stuck at 63%, suggesting price correction ahead. A100 at $0.93/hr is undervalued for inference shops running Llama 70B, where TCO-adjusted cost yields 21% margin at high-tier pricing versus negative margins on H100 and H200.
bullets: 37% util-avail on H200 means over a third of deployed capacity is idle despite a 10.3% overnight spot price spike.
35% util-avail on H100 reflects a 25.3pp utilization drop over 30 days, the second steepest decline across GPU classes.
$0.15 per million output tokens is what OpenAI charges for gpt-oss-120b, a 99.75% deflation from GPT-4 launch pricing.
65% of advertised B200 offerings on NeoCloud platforms sit unrented, the highest vacancy rate of any GPU class.
$3.66 per million tokens is the estimated loss DeepSeek eats on every V3.2 output call versus TCO-adjusted B200 cost.
127.2% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable GPU contract, more than double B200 volatility.
